gaps.title: Telegram Account Session and Proxy Manager
gaps.jtbd: When an administrator manages the outbound messaging infrastructure, I want to authenticate Telegram accounts using OTP or session files and route all traffic through isolated proxies, so that we can monitor session health and avoid chain bans.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a Svelte dashboard and a Spring Boot backend, When the administrator enters an OTP code or uploads a session file with a dedicated proxy, Then the backend successfully establishes a secure TDLib/Telegram connection and tracks its online/banned status in real time.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 1 (Session Manager), including account authorization (OTP/session upload), individual proxy binding, status-monitor, and anti-fraud simulation, is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Campaign Management and Spintax Template Engine
gaps.jtbd: When a marketing operator configures an outreach campaign, I want to upload contact lists and define randomized message templates using spintax, so that campaigns can be launched automatically across rotated accounts without triggering signature-based spam filters.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a campaign CSV/TXT file with contacts and a spintax template, When the operator initiates the campaign, Then the backend parses contacts, generates randomized message variations, and schedules dispatch with appropriate delays.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 2 (Campaign Manager) is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Unified Live Chat CRM and Manual Control Takeover
gaps.jtbd: When an operator monitors ongoing conversations, I want a unified chat interface with color-coded status markings and instant manual message capabilities, so that I can immediately take over control from the AI when human attention is required.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a unified live chat panel, When an operator views active dialogues, Then they are displayed with appropriate color coding for their states, and sending a message manually halts future AI auto-responses for that dialogue.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 4 (Диалоговая Панель / Live Chat CRM) is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Account Warming and Exponentially Distributed Timeouts
gaps.jtbd: When operating a network of Telegram outreach accounts, I want to simulate background activity (e.g., channel subscriptions, reading posts) and apply exponentially distributed delay intervals, so that the risk of automated spam blocks is minimized.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a Telegram account session, When background warming is active, Then it performs periodic automated actions and randomizes delays using an exponential distribution rather than fixed pauses.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Section 4 outlines Session Warmup and dynamic timeouts as critical anti-ban strategy features, which are not addressed by any task in the list.

gaps.title: Administrator Authentication and Session Management
gaps.jtbd: When an administrator accesses the self-hosted platform, I want to authenticate securely via a login screen, so that unauthorized external users cannot access or control the active Telegram sessions and campaign data.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given an unauthenticated request to the Svelte admin dashboard or Spring Boot API, When the request is made, Then it is redirected to a login page or returns an HTTP 401 Unauthorized, requiring valid administrator credentials to proceed.
gaps.reason: domain-standard expectation: A private self-hosted admin panel must have secure user authentication and session management to prevent unauthorized access, which is also implied by 'Spring Security' and the 'Users' table in the brief's conceptual DB schema.
